# OpenClaw Adapter

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/providermigrationdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to Public Migration Status](../public-migration-status.md)

RIID-4660 moved internal/provider/openclaw into public riido-daemon.

The package does not bundle OpenClaw CLI.

Facts:

- It owns external executable detection, calendar-version gate, openclaw agent --local --json command construction, mandatory session id resolution, JSON/NDJSON parser, and raw event translator.
- Real OpenClaw execution is gated by AGENTBRIDGE_INTEGRATION=1.
- OpenClaw --session-id is provider-native.
- Existing ResumeSessionID is preserved, but first-run TaskID fallback is normalized to riido-<sanitized-task-slug>-<short-hash>.
- A-42 showed SaaS thread completion can happen without expected workdir artifact, so text completion is not filesystem side-effect evidence.
- A-48 added an integration gate that expects ResultCompleted and an expected file artifact in daemon-selected workdir.
- If the gate skips, filesystem side effect is not proven.
- OpenClaw agent currently lacks per-run --workspace / --cwd.
- Tasks requiring daemon-selected workdir are pre-submit blocked with required_surfaces=["worktree"].
- Capability reconciliation exposes OpenClaw SupportsWorktree=false and Claude/Codex/Cursor SupportsWorktree=true.

Evidence artifacts:

- internal/provider/openclaw
- .github/workflows/provider-openclaw.yml
